blocks.content: Eli was at arrivals with coffee and the expression of a man participating in something historic.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Nobody knows you're here. I told Savannah you were staying in LA.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Mrs. Jones thinks I'm bringing her a casserole dish I borrowed. She has no idea.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I'm going to show up at the door. I want to catch her real.
Eli looked at him over the roof of the car.

Millhaven in October was all copper and gold, the trees doing their annual slow fire, the air carrying that first edge of cold that Georgia only got for about six weeks before winter gave up. He'd forgotten how beautiful it was. LA had its own beauty but it didn't look like this — didn't smell like this, didn't have this particular quality of light that hit the old houses at four in the afternoon like something out of a painting.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I want to start at the street.
Eli drove them there without another word.
